title: Move SVA code out of Verific driver into core?
body:
[Antmicro's recent blog post](https://antmicro.com/blog/2022/02/simplifying-open-source-sv-synthesis-with-the-yosys-uhdm-plugin/) about integrating the up-and-coming Surelog SystemVerilog parser prompted me to kick its tires.  The parser's language support is very good and for the first time it's meaningful to talk about how a "common core" of SVA support would look that facilitates both Verific and other frontends.  It would be unspeakably fantastic to have SVA support in combination with Yosys's formal tooling.

Here's a simple module for reference; Surelog correctly emits a parse tree for this.

```systemverilog
module very_simple(input clk, input data);
    assert property( @(posedge clk) data |=> ~data);
endmodule
```

(Perhaps you'd `bind` this into your design, which works out of the box with Surelog!)

I decided to look at adding SVA support to AntMicro's plugin, but ran into a roadblock: it appears that most of Yosys's support for SVA is actually present in the frontend - e.g. `|=>` gets lowered to a state machine in this section:

https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/55e8f5061af57bf25bd9e30528de8196c6eabe9e/frontends/verific/verificsva.cc#L1560-L1567

(I don't have a Verific-enabled version to try so it's possible I could be misinterpreting.)

Much of this looks like it should be common across any set of parsers that supply an SV AST - if I provide a "concurrent assertion" syntax tree, it can also be lowered to an FSM.  Is it feasible to move this functionality out of the Verific section?
